name: Natural Park Montes de Málaga – running circular by Los Torrijos
name_original: Parque Natural Montes de Málaga – running circular por Los Torrijos
region_intro: Jogging in Montes De Málaga offers diverse landscapes across nearly 5,000 hectares, characterized by rugged terrain, uneven mountain ranges, and valleys. The area features extensive Aleppo pine forests, a result of reforestation, alongside remnants of original Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and cork oaks. Joggers will find a network of paths and forest tracks, including challenging ascents and gentler routes, often with views of the Mediterranean Sea. This natural park provides a refreshing escape with varied topography for all running levels. Best jogging routes in Montes De Málaga The most popular jogging route is Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes 32 minutes to complete. This easy circular route offers a pleasant run near the forest park. Another top favourite among local runners is Natural Park Montes de Málaga – running circular by Los Torrijos, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route provides a good workout within the natural park, featuring varied terrain. Local runners also love the Lagar de Pocopán Viewpoint – Puerto del León loop from Parque Natural Montes de Málaga, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail leading through the challenging mountain landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Jogging in Montes De Málaga is defined by dense pine forests, varied mountain terrain, and panoramic viewpoints. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Montes De Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 27000 runners have used komoot to explore Montes De Málaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Montes de Málaga?
A: Montes de Málaga offers a vast network of over 230 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy loops to challenging long-distance trails within the natural park.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Montes de Málaga has several easy running options. For a pleasant and accessible run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996222837/embalse-el-limonero-circular-by-the-la-concepcion-forest-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.0-mile (4.8 km) trail. The Torrijos Trail, less than a kilometer, is also very easy and starts near the eco-museum.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes and features can I expect to see while running?
A: While running in Montes de Málaga, you'll experience diverse landscapes including dense Aleppo pine forests, rugged mountain ranges, and valleys carved by streams. You can also find remnants of original Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and cork oaks. The park is home to various wildlife, including birds of prey, wild boars, and foxes. Many routes offer panoramic views of the natural park, Málaga city, and the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting along the running paths?
A: Absolutely! Montes de Málaga is known for its stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/132339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; (Mirador del Cochino) offers impressive vistas, and on clear days, you might even glimpse the African coast. Other notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1006359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Francisco Vázquez Sell Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and Mirador de Martínez Falero, providing breathtaking panoramic views of the park and the sea.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the park?
A: Yes, many routes in Montes de Málaga are circular, offering a convenient way to explore without retracing your steps. Popular options include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996222837/embalse-el-limonero-circular-by-the-la-concepcion-forest-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse El Limonero – circular by the La Concepción Forest Park&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996225864/natural-park-montes-de-malaga-running-circular-by-los-torrijos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park Montes de Málaga – running circular by Los Torrijos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there public transport access to the Montes de Málaga running trails?
A: While the park is just 5 kilometers north of Málaga city, public transport options directly into the heart of the trail network can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes that might pass closer to the park's entrances or specific trailheads. Many visitors opt for private vehicles for easier access.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the natural park for a run?
A: There are designated parking areas at various access points and trailheads within Montes de Málaga Natural Park. For example, the Torrijos Trail starts at the old Torrijos winery, which has facilities including a visitor's center, likely with parking. Specific viewpoints and popular trail starting points often have parking available.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Montes de Málaga?
A: The best time to go running in Montes de Málaga is generally during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). The dense pine forests offer shade, but summer temperatures can be very high. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn provides pleasant temperatures and beautiful foliage. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

Q: What do other runners say about their experience in Montes de Málaga?
A: The running routes in Montes de Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the refreshing escape from the city, and the stunning panoramic views. The extensive network of paths ensures options for all abilities, making it a popular choice for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls or historical sites?
A: Yes, the Picapedreros-Boticario Trail is a pleasant 7-kilometer route that passes by the Picapedreros waterfall. This trail also showcases the region's wine-growing past with ruins of old wineries. Additionally, the park features ancient prehistoric necropolises and historical structures like traditional dry stone walls, adding cultural interest to your run.

Q: What are some longer, more challenging running options in the park?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21594531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagar de Pocopán Viewpoint – Puerto del León loop from Parque Natural Montes de Málaga&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 13.9-mile (22.4 km) trail through the mountainous landscape. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31828092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pocopán Viewpoint – Francisco Vázquez Sell Viewpoint loop from Venta El Mijeño&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 39 kilometers with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find routes with diverse tree species for a varied running experience?
A: Absolutely. While Aleppo pine forests are dominant, the Pocopán Trail is particularly known for its diverse trees, offering a route through pine, holm oak, and cork oak forests. This provides a varied and scenic running experience, ideal for those who appreciate different types of woodland.
tips: Nice and quiet place with beautiful views towards the interior of the Montes de Málaga. You will find some benches and an indicative sign to identify what you see.

The Mirador Francisco Vázquez Sell is a beautiful spot in the natural environment of the Montes de Málaga. It is a viewpoint named after Francisco Vázquez Sell, an engineer who played an important role in the recovery of the water and forest areas in the Guadalmedina River Valley.
From the viewpoint you can enjoy beautiful panoramic views, including the Bay of Málaga, the mountains of Mijas, Alhaurín and Cártama, and the Sierra de las Nieves Natural Park. It is a great place to walk and enjoy nature.
